issue.title: Lane refill: eliminate remaining Python test files and harness residue

commands: for path in tests tests/conftest.py tests/test_connectors.py tests/test_console_ia.py tests/test_execution_contract.py tests/test_execution_flow.py tests/test_followup_digests.py tests/test_governance.py tests/test_models.py tests/test_observability.py tests/test_reports.py scripts/ops/bigclaw_workspace_bootstrap.py scripts/ops/symphony_workspace_bootstrap.py; do test ! -e "$path" && printf 'absent %s\n' "$path"; done

results.assigned_residue_absence: absent tests; absent tests/conftest.py; absent tests/test_connectors.py; absent tests/test_console_ia.py; absent tests/test_execution_contract.py; absent tests/test_execution_flow.py; absent tests/test_followup_digests.py; absent tests/test_governance.py; absent tests/test_models.py; absent tests/test_observability.py; absent tests/test_reports.py; absent scripts/ops/bigclaw_workspace_bootstrap.py; absent scripts/ops/symphony_workspace_bootstrap.py

blocker.summary: The repository-wide physical Python file count was already zero in this workspace before BIG-GO-1604 changes.

notes: This lane documents the final retired root tests slice and bootstrap/harness residue as already absent, removes the last standalone Python-test regression file, and hardens the remaining state with Go regression coverage.
The branch baseline was already Python-free, so this lane strengthens regression coverage and refreshes validation evidence rather than removing in-branch Python source files.
The remaining `python_*`-named Go regression files stay in place because active zero-Python guards still reference them.
Baseline HEAD before lane changes was `23addc82`.
